_README: MmapGen per-map / per-tile generator config. Top-level keys are stringified map IDs; values for that map are flat scalars (agentRadius, agentHeight, walkableRadius, walkableHeight, walkableSlopeAngle, etc.). Per-tile overrides go in nested objects under the map (the inner key is `tileX || tileY`, no separator). The `default` block at the top is read by tools/NavDataAudit only -- MmapGen itself ignores it. See docs/physics/MMAP_FORMAT.md.
_agentNotes: Tauren Male is the largest WoW capsule (radius 1.0247, height 2.625). All continent maps must be generated for that capsule. agentRadius / agentHeight are in WoW meters. walkableRadius/walkableHeight/walkableClimb were previously specified as integer cell counts tied to ch=0.25 / cs=0.2666; that is a footgun when ch or cs change (PFS-OVERHAUL-006: setting them to 0 lets TileWorker auto-derive cell counts from agentRadius/agentHeight/agentMaxClimbModelTerrainTransition divided by cs/ch, producing correct world-unit clearance / climb regardless of cell size). Per-tile walkableErosionRadius is a separate source-support radius for rcErodeWalkableArea only; it does not change the Detour/header agent capsule.

0._3345_README: BRD/BRM route tile for Flame Crest -> BRD approach / upper-spire portal cluster. Apply only source-support erosion tuning here; do not lower climb or add route repair.
0._3446_NEGATIVE_RESULT: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 BRM Round-4-redo (2026-05-09): tested per-tile agentMaxClimbTerrain overrides for tile filename 0004634.mmtile (CLI 34,46) at values 1.0 and 0.2. 1.0 had negligible effect (Walk count 299->296). 0.2 was destructive: Walk count dropped to 13/1071 because Detour rerouted around the over-fragmented tile via a worse cliff corridor. The OG-zeppelin Cycle-17e fix worked because the over-collapsed polygon was a shortcut; disconnecting it forced the bot onto the legitimate spiral ramp inside the same tile. BRM south-face has no equivalent legitimate alternate corridor inside (46,34); aggressive disconnection just pushes Detour to neighbouring tiles whose own cliffs are worse. Single-tile config is the wrong lever for this geometry; needs either coordinated multi-tile bake (tighter walkableClimb across 46,28..36 simultaneously) or a different bake parameter (for example walkableHeight or filterLedgeSpans tuning) that fragments cliff-edge cells without destroying horizontal connectivity. See memory project_pfs_overhaul_006_pathphysicsprobe_brm_route.md for the probe-driven evidence and project_pfs_overhaul_006_brm_singletile_negative.md for this round.
0._3446_README_erosion: 2026-05-13: try the OG-style source-support erosion split without changing Detour/header capsule or climb. walkableErosionRadius=0.2y trims rcErodeWalkableArea only; it is meant to preserve thin WMO/source surfaces for the BRD run while avoiding the earlier climb-fragmentation regression.
0._3546_README_erosion: 2026-05-13: live Flame Crest -> BRD/UBRS/LBRS/BWL tests stall around (-7519,-2100,130), which maps to MmapGen tile 35,46 / runtime 0004635.mmtile. Apply the same source-support erosion split and export a focused stall crop before investigating BotRunner execution.
0._3546_README_slope_negative_iter2: 2026-05-13 (terrain-only retry, REVERTED again): a follow-up attempt set walkableSlopeAngle=52 on terrain (vmap left at default 61) to address the FG run regression from the both-sides-52 attempt. Live result: the bot escaped the original stall coord (-7519,-2100,130) but progressed only as far as (-7665,-1808,137) in Ruins of Thaurissan, where it pressed nose-first into a BRM rock wall while the pathfinder service returned route=none for replan attempts (plan=33..37 all returned no route). The user-supplied screenshot showed the bot pinned against a near-vertical rock face. The stuck detector did NOT catch the stall because the bot crept ~0.25y/s along the wall and the existing SnapshotStallGuard threshold (1.5y over 45s) was defeated by that creep. Reverted again. Next iteration must either (a) implement a runtime NAV_STEEP_SLOPES exclude in Exports/Navigation/PathFinder.cpp::createFilter (vmangos's Map.cpp pattern) so the mesh stays as-is and player paths skip steep-slope polys regardless of the bake's classification — leaves models intact, requires Navigation.dll + Docker rebuild — or (b) add an off-mesh connection for the BRM ascent so Detour has an explicit route up the mountain. The bake-level slope tightening is the wrong fix surface; both tried versions broke something else.
0._3446_NEGATIVE_RESULT_surface_F: PFS-OVERHAUL BRM ascent Phase 2 Surface F (2026-05-14, REVERTED): tried per-tile maxSimplificationError=0.5 → 'Too many vertices' overbuild (Detour's 16-bit vertex limit); fell back to 1.2 → tile baked (3020100 → 3619812 bytes, +20%) and Walkable_AllFourPortals_HaveGroundPoly flipped GREEN (ubrs_portal poly captured the queried XY this time). BUT the bake's denser polygon graph (corridor poly count 287→355 on FC→UBRS) caused TWO regressions on previously-GREEN gates — Corridor_FcToUbrsPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly and Corridor_FcToLbrsPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly both ended at NEW polyIdxes that don't match the portal polys (the upper-cluster polygon IDs all shifted under the tighter simplification). On top of that, SmoothPath_FcToUbrsPortal_NoUnreasonableZJump hung >9min (vs <100ms baseline) — catastrophic A* perf explosion. Net 11/14 → 10/14 and a live-FG-fatal query-time regression. The polygon-footprint-short-of-queried-XY hypothesis was correct but maxSimplificationError is the wrong knob: making the bake denser shifts every polygon's ID and explodes A* without a path to the literal portal coord. Future attempts: try walkableErosionRadius=0.0 (eliminate erosion margin entirely so polygon footprints extend to the source walkable boundary) or pursue Surface G/H first; revisit F if those don't expose a different fix surface.
0._3446_NEGATIVE_RESULT_surface_G: PFS-OVERHAUL BRM ascent Phase 2 Surface G (2026-05-14, REVERTED): tried per-tile walkableErosionRadius=0.0 (vs 0.2 baseline) to eliminate rcErodeWalkableArea margin and extend polygon footprints toward source walkable boundary. Hypothesis was that erosion-only changes wouldn't split polys (stable polyIdxes). FALSIFIED — the 0.0 bake produced erosionRadiusCells=0 (vs 1 baseline) and tile grew 3020100 → 3729412 bytes (+23%, comparable to Surface F's 1.2). Property tests (prod-data): Walkable_AllFourPortals_HaveGroundPoly GREEN (side-effect, same as Surface F mse=1.2), but Corridor_FcToBwlPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly STILL RED (corridor 1 poly short — term=0x14F02470 vs portal=0x14F024F1, just different polyIdxes from baseline) AND Corridor_FcToUbrsPortal/FcToLbrsPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly BOTH REGRESSED (was GREEN, now ends at 0x14F047DB which doesn't match new UBRS/LBRS portal polys 0x14F046E8 / 0x14F041A8) AND SmoothPath_FcToUbrsPortal_NoUnreasonableZJump HUNG >9min (same A* explosion as Surface F). Net 11/14 → 9/14 + fatal A* perf regression. Root cause clarified: ANY bake-parameter change to tile 3446 that touches the walkable-cell map (mse, erosionRadius, ch, cs, walkableSlopeAngle...) shifts the entire polygon graph because rcBuildRegions/rcBuildContours/rcBuildPolyMesh are deterministic from the walkable input but sensitive to small perturbations. The BWL corridor 1-poly-short symptom is robust to bake-knob tightening on this tile — fundamental tile-3446 connectivity gap, not a bake-fidelity issue. Surface H targets a DIFFERENT tile (Ruins of Thaurissan ascending corridor) and may not have this problem. After F and G negative, the structural conclusion is that tile 3446's corridor-terminus failures need a different fix surface than tile-3446 bake knobs — candidates: off-mesh connections (would need Phase 4 of overhaul), or recognizing the BWL/UBRS/LBRS portal polys have intentional in-game connectivity gaps that the bake cannot recover from generic terrain processing.
0._3546_README_surface_H: PFS-OVERHAUL BRM ascent Phase 2 Surface H (2026-05-14): tighten per-tile cs from default 0.2666 to 0.15 to fragment the 2.61y-dz polygon at WP[259] (-7673.9,-1746.2,136.0)→(-7679.0,-1747.5,133.4) into shorter steps. Per the cs+tileSize coupling rule (memory project_pfs_overhaul_006_config_key_inversion): when cs shrinks, tileSize must grow so 25*tileSize*cs ≈ 533.33y. For cs=0.15: tileSize = 533.33/(25*0.15) ≈ 142.22 → use 142. OG zeppelin precedent: cs=0.1 paired with tileSize=213 (25*213*0.1 = 532.5y). Target gate: SmoothPath_FcToUbrsPortal_NoUnreasonableZJump (4 violations, worst 2.61y at idx 259). Tile 3546 is the Flame Crest stall tile, a DIFFERENT tile from F/G's 3446 — so the tile-3446 corridor-terminus tests won't see polyIdx churn.

1._4029_README: Per-tile override for the Orgrimmar zeppelin tower tile. The vmangos tile frame is swapped relative to normal WoW XYZ: tileX is derived from world Y and tileY is derived from world X. CLI invocation is `MmapGen.exe 1 --tile 40,29` and writes `mmaps/0012940.mmtile` (`sprintf` order is mapId, tileY, tileX). `TileWorker::getTileConfig` looks up the nested key as `tileX || tileY`, so the live OG dock key is `"4029"`. The older `2940`/`0014029` notes were wrong-tile artifacts.
1._4029_README_REVALIDATE: These knobs were originally discovered while the visualization/docs had the tile axes reversed. They are carried forward to the corrected Orgrimmar tile because the design intent still belongs here, but the next regen must revalidate them against fresh 40,29 geometry before treating them as proven.
1._4029_README_cs: cs=0.1m (vs 0.2666m default) for fine horizontal resolution on thin overhanging structures (the deck edge wall + railing). Tile size grows with cs=0.1, but only for this one tile.
1._4029_README_ledgefilter: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 / Phase 6 per-tile ledge-filter overrides. treatOobNeighborAsCliff=false: dock cells whose voxel-column neighbors are out-of-tile-bounds (OG harbor edge) stop counting OOB as an infinite drop, so the ledge filter does not reject them. mixedAreaUsesTerrainClimb=true: dock-edge cells transitioning to model decorations on top keep using the 1.8y terrain climb instead of the tighter about-1.2y model-transition climb. Both flags default to legacy behavior in getDefaultConfig() so non-opted tiles are unaffected. See TileWorker.cpp::filterLedgeSpans.
1._4029_README_climb: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 Cycle 17 / Intervention 3 intended to disconnect the polygon corridor's lower-platform -> upper-deck shortcut at the OG zeppelin tower deck-lip. Default walkableClimbTerrain=1.8y is the harvested-from-client baseline; here we tighten to 0.2y for tile (40,29) ONLY. Revalidate with corrected 40,29 source geometry before promoting this to a closed fix.
1._4029_README_debug: debugStageCropWow is a focused MmapGen debug export crop for the Orgrimmar tower top ramp/deck. It writes stable heightfield, compact heightfield, and contour CSV stage files only while --debug is used.
1._4029_README_polys: maxVertsPerPoly=3 is a focused bake-quality probe/fix for the tower top-ramp/deck crop. The previous generator ignored this key; TileWorker now honors it so the OG deck can be tested without route-time repair.
1._4029_README_erosion: walkableErosionRadius=0.2y separates WMO floor-support erosion from the Tauren collision capsule. The Detour tile still advertises agentRadius=1.0247y / agentHeight=2.625y, but rcErodeWalkableArea no longer erases thin top-deck connector slabs that FG screenshots show characters can stand on. A 0.0y probe overbuilt the tile and exceeded Detour's 16-bit vertex limit.
